assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md: 'Classification: code change request'. Action: 'Sub-task created to wrap the three UPDATE operations in soft_delete inside a database transaction.' subtask-30001.md exists. report.md line 131: '| 30001 | modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs:60 | code change request | Sub-task created |'

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion -- the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md: 'Classification: suggestion'. Classification reasoning explicitly identifies suggestive phrasing: '"should also add" -- advisory tone, not a directive requirement' and '"would help" -- conditional language indicating this is a recommendation, not a mandate'. The reasoning explains why this is a suggestion rather than a code change request.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md: 'Classification: nit'. Action: 'No sub-task created.' No subtask-30003.md file exists. report.md line 133: '| 30003 | modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs:18 | nit | No sub-task created |'

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md: 'Classification: question'. Action: 'No sub-task created.' No subtask-30004.md file exists. report.md line 134: '| 30004 | modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/get.rs:1 | question | No sub-task created |'

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1), '## Description' (line 7), '## Files to Modify' (line 11), '## Implementation Notes' (line 14), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 21). Only one sub-task was created (subtask-30001.md), so 'each' applies only to it.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' (lines 29-30).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' section (lines 32-37) with Comment ID: 30001, Reviewer: reviewer-a, File path, and the full original comment text: 'The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files -- it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only markdown files: report.md, review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md, subtask-30001.md. No source code files (.rs, .toml, .yaml, etc.) were created or appear in the outputs. All output files are classification reports, sub-task descriptions, or the verification report.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have individual classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md). The report.md Review Feedback Classification table (lines 129-134) lists all 4 comments with their classifications. report.md line 5: '4 review comments classified: 1 code change request, 1 suggestion, 1 nit, 1 question'.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains no mention of merging, auto-merge, or any merge action. The overall result is 'WARN' (line 17), and the report concludes with a note about the code change request requiring a sub-task. No merge-related commands or actions appear anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE -- only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file adding 5 test functions |'. Section 'Test Change Classification -- ADDITIVE' (lines 122-123): 'tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file (not present on the base branch). New test files are inherently additive.'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) -- the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a detailed 'Convention upgrade eligibility evaluation' section (lines 13-28) that checks: 1) CONVENTIONS.md for documented convention, 2) Codebase pattern check for demonstrated patterns, 3) Performance-related scrutiny. The report.md 'Convention Upgrade -- PASS' section (lines 94-100) also evaluates comment 30002 and explains why no upgrade occurred.

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task -- the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: No subtask-30002.md file exists in the outputs directory. review-30002.md concludes: 'The comment remains classified as suggestion. No sub-task created.' report.md line 132: '| 30002 | migration/src/m0042_sbom_soft_delete/mod.rs:14 | suggestion | No sub-task created |'. The upgrade decision explicitly states: 'The suggestion is NOT upgraded because: No matching convention exists in CONVENTIONS.md..., No demonstrated codebase pattern..., General database best practices are explicitly insufficient for upgrade.'

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR -- the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: 'Eval Quality: N/A'. Lines 117-119: 'No eval result reviews found on the PR (no reviews matching all three detection criteria: author `github-actions[bot]`, body containing `## Eval Results`, and body containing `sdlc-workflow/run-evals`).' The Test Quality row (line 13) shows 'PASS' overall with Eval Quality as N/A, confirming it does not affect the combination.
